description:
**fz-dps.C4**: lower Term::TailCall / Term::TailCallClosure under Dst ABI.

**What**: at a tail callsite where caller's own return-repr is `Dst(k)` and tail callee's return-repr is the same `Dst(k)`, forward caller's dst arg unchanged to callee. No new alloc.

**Why this lands before q9g.3**: tail recursion in DPS-shaped constructors (the canonical TRMC pattern) requires per-iteration dst forwarding. Without C4, the recursive arm of `map`/`reverse_acc` cannot complete the chain when q9g.3 flips the gate.

**Interaction with `is_pure_tail_caller`** (src/ir_inline.rs:36, landed in PR #14): for small-K shapes `K(x) -> TailCall(target, [box(x)])`, the inliner already eats the K hop before C4 sees it. C4 still fires for: K above INLINE_BUDGET, K shared across multiple callsites, K recursive or referenced by MakeClosure. Test fixture must pick a multi-callsite or above-budget K so the demonstration isn't pre-empted upstream.

**Testability without classifier output**: same shape as q9g.4 — test toggles q9g.2's gate under `cfg(test)`, synthesizes a Dst spec with a tail-recursive call, asserts the lowered TailCall forwards the dst arg.

**CLIF impact**: zero in production builds; test-only assertions verify lowering shape.

**Precondition**: q9g.4 (C3 must exist — the non-tail path is the regression baseline TailCall measures against).

comments.text: subsumed by fz-q9g.3 (CD-bundle): tail-forward dst meaningless without producer/consumer wire

title: fz-dps.C4: lower Term::TailCall/TailCallClosure under Dst ABI
